title: Add mutex to JSON session registry
description:
BUG: JSON file registry vulnerable to race conditions. Even single-process Node with Express can interleave requests:
1. Request A reads file
2. Request B reads file
3. A writes new state
4. B writes based on old state, clobbering A

Fix options:
1. In-process mutex around load-modify-save
2. Move sessions to SQLite (better-sqlite3 already shipped)
3. In-memory authoritative map with single serialized write queue

Files: src/registry/session-registry.js
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Superseded by Pigeon Phase 2 storage plan (ccr-c97): daemon migration is SQLite-first, replacing JSON registry concurrency concerns.

description: Break 776-line handler into NotificationSender, WebhookReceiver, CommandRouter, TokenManager. Reference: docs/plans/2026-02-09-handler-decomposition.md

title: SQLite storage consolidation (replace JSON files)
description: Replace daemon's JSON file storage backends with single SQLite database. Sessions and tokens currently use JSON files with async-mutex. Migrate to SQLite for atomic ops, indexed lookups, WAL concurrency. Reference: docs/plans/2026-02-09-sqlite-consolidation.md

close_reason: 13 webhook tests. Auth, dedup, allowlist, reply/cmd/callback routing. fetchMock patterns established.

title: Revive nvim injection + move plugins into pigeon
description:
Move ccremote.lua (renamed pigeon.lua) and Claude hooks into pigeon repo. Implement NvimRpcAdapter behind a shared CommandDeliveryAdapter interface. Refactor direct-channel to use the same interface. Update workstation to consume from pigeon.

Use case: Multiple Claude Code sessions inside nvim terminal buffers in one tmux pane. Only nvim chansend(job_id) can target the right buffer; tmux send-keys can't distinguish them. PTY-keyed auto-registration maps each session's TTY to the correct nvim buffer.

References:
- ccremote.lua: workstation/assets/nvim/lua/ccremote.lua (326 lines)
- Claude hooks: workstation/assets/claude/hooks/
- Architecture plan: claude-code-remote/docs/plans/2026-02-09-nvim-rpc-auto-registration.md
close_reason: Duplicate epic, keeping ccr-4t0

Just nvim_socket — no transport_kind or other old bloat. pty_path already exists.

title: Fix Markdown parse_mode reliability
description:
BUG: Notifications use parse_mode: 'Markdown'. If Claude output contains Telegram markdown control characters, message is rejected and returns 502.

Fix options:
1. Don't use parse_mode (safest)
2. Escape text properly before sending
3. Use MarkdownV2 with strict escaping

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (sendTelegramMessage calls)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Superseded by Pigeon worker rewrite and Phase 2 daemon notification work; parse_mode handling is no longer tracked in legacy worker path.

title: Add WebSocket authentication for machine connections
description:
SECURITY CRITICAL: WebSocket endpoint /ws?machineId=X has no authentication. Anyone can:
- Connect as any machineId
- Receive commands intended for that machine
- Send fake commandResult responses
- Impersonate machines to spam Telegram via DO

Fix options:
1. Require auth token in Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header
2. Require first message to be {type:'auth', token:'...'}, close if not received in N seconds
3. Per-machine keys in CCR_MACHINE_KEYS env var

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (handleWebSocket)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Completed: WebSocket auth with first-message protocol

title: Update session updated_at on activity
description:
BUG: sessions.updated_at only set on register. Long-running sessions (24h+) get cleaned up even if actively in use.

Fix: Update updated_at on meaningful activity:
- On every routed command
- On every notification  
- On periodic machine heartbeat

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (routeCommand, handleNotification, consider adding heartbeat)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Superseded by Pigeon worker rewrite: session updated_at is touched on command activity in webhook routing flow.

title: Add Telegram update deduplication
description:
BUG: Telegram can retry webhooks if response is slow/errors. Current code does network calls inline (sendTelegramMessage, answerCallbackQuery). If Telegram times out and retries:
- Duplicate commands enqueued
- Duplicate 'queued' messages sent

Fix:
1. Respond 200 OK fast, push work into state.waitUntil()
2. Dedupe by update.update_id or (chat_id, message_id) - track seen updates

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (handleTelegramWebhook)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Superseded by Pigeon worker rewrite: Telegram webhook deduplication implemented via seen_updates table in TypeScript worker.

title: Add ack-based command queue deletion
description:
BUG: Commands deleted from queue immediately after ws.send(), with no ack. If socket dies mid-flush, commands are LOST.

Current: send() then DELETE immediately
Problem: No guarantee message was received

Fix options (increasing robustness):
1. Wrap send+delete in try/catch, stop on send failure
2. Add ack protocol: DO sends {type:'command', id}, machine replies {type:'ack', id}, DO deletes on ack
3. Add per-machine max queue length to prevent unbounded growth

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (flushCommandQueue)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Superseded by Pigeon worker rewrite: command queue now uses persisted pending/sent/acked lifecycle with ack handling and alarm cleanup in packages/worker/src/command-queue.ts.

title: Revive nvim RPC injection with PTY-keyed auto-registration
description:
Bring back nvim RPC injection for Claude Code sessions, using PTY-based auto-registration from the Feb 9 architecture plan.

Use case: Multiple CC sessions inside one tmux pane (nvim terminals). tmux send-keys can't distinguish buffers; only nvim chansend(job_id) can target the right one.

Architecture:
- CommandDeliveryAdapter interface with deliverCommand(session, command) -> {ok, error?}
  - DirectChannelAdapter (OpenCode, already exists)
  - NvimRpcAdapter (Claude Code, new)
- Session model: add pty_path (for keying) + nvim_socket (for RPC). Drop old transport_kind/pane_id/session_name bloat.
- ccremote.lua auto-registers terminal buffers by PTY on TermOpen, deregisters on TermClose
- Session-start hook sends TTY path; daemon maps PTY -> nvim job_id
- Adapter: connect to nvim socket, find channel by PTY, chansend command

Reference: /home/dev/projects/claude-code-remote/docs/plans/2026-02-09-nvim-rpc-auto-registration.md
close_reason: Replacing with proper epic + subtasks

Harness covers session start registration, stop notification, webhook reply+callback routing, worker command delivery, local injection, and session unregister cleanup. Dispatches base64-encoded JSON payload to pigeon.lua's dispatch function. Handles: connection timeout, nvim not responding, instance not found (PTY not registered). Returns {ok, error?}.

title: Add Nix derivation for opencode-pigeon plugin bundle
description: Create a Nix derivation that bundles the opencode-pigeon plugin (packages/opencode-plugin/src/index.ts) into a single JS file using bun build, and wire it into home-manager via xdg.configFile to deploy to ~/.config/opencode/plugins/opencode-pigeon.js. Currently we use a dev symlink to the repo source, which works but breaks if the repo is not present or the path changes. The derivation should: (1) run bun build --target=bun --format=esm --external=@opencode-ai/plugin --external=@opencode-ai/sdk to produce a single bundled file, (2) resolve the cross-package import from ../../daemon/src/opencode-direct/contracts at build time, (3) produce a stable artifact for home-manager to symlink.

title: Add test coverage for worker Durable Object (router-do)
description: 1084-line production Durable Object has zero tests. Extract pure business logic into testable functions, add integration tests via Miniflare. 15+ API endpoints to cover. Reference: docs/plans/2026-02-09-worker-test-coverage.md

title: Fix cross-chat token replay vulnerability
description:
SECURITY: Token lookups in /cmd and callbacks don't bind to chat_id. If a token leaks, someone can DM the bot from ANY chat and route commands to that session.

Current (vulnerable):
  SELECT session_id FROM messages WHERE token = ?

Fixed:
  SELECT session_id FROM messages WHERE token = ? AND chat_id = ?

Also consider storing/verifying from_user_id.

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (handleTelegramWebhook, routeCommand, handleCallback)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Completed: Token routing bound to chat_id

close_reason: Implemented session intake API parity on new SQLite-backed daemon: /session-start, /sessions list/detail, /sessions/enable-notify, DELETE /sessions/:id, and /cleanup with legacy-compatible status/response shapes. Added parity tests. Pushed in pigeon commit 7bcbc4a.

title: Structured error types with retryable flag
description: Replace generic Error with typed error classes (TelegramApiError, SessionNotFoundError, InjectionError) with retryable flag and error code. Enables smart retry logic. Reference: docs/plans/2026-02-09-borrowed-patterns.md

title: Add Telegram chat/user allowlist
description:
SECURITY: No access control on who can interact with the bot. If bot is discoverable or added to groups, anyone can attempt to route commands.

Fix: Add ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS and ALLOWED_USER_IDS env vars. Reject requests from unlisted sources early in webhook handler. Optionally reject group chats unless explicitly enabled.

Files: ccr-worker/src/router-do.js (handleTelegramWebhook - add early check)
Ref: ChatGPT code review 2026-01-22
close_reason: Completed: Telegram chat/user allowlist (fail-closed)

title: Daemon injection adapters (nvim + tmux)
description: Port and harden nvim RPC injector plus tmux fallback adapter in modular TS form. Preserve command injection behavior and failure handling parity.
close_reason: Implemented nvim and tmux injection adapters with factory selection and nvim->tmux fallback semantics, integrated into worker command ingest path, with dedicated adapter tests. Pushed in commit 84c8848.
